  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_ai", "name": "Artificial intelligence", "aliases": ["AI"], "description": "Technology described as evolving at a rapid pace with complexities, risks, and societal consequences for federal use.", "grounding_span": "Artificial intelligence (AI) is evolving at a rapid pace", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "c_ai_accountability_framework", "name": "AI Accountability Framework", "aliases": ["Artificial Intelligence (AI) Accountability Framework"], "description": "GAO framework of key practices to help ensure accountability and responsible AI use, organized around four principles.", "grounding_span": "GAO developed an AI Accountability Framework of key practices to help ensure responsible AI use by federal agencies", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "c_accountability", "name": "Accountability", "aliases": [], "description": "The end the framework is designed to help ensure in federal use of AI.", "grounding_span": "Key Practices to Help Ensure Accountability in Federal Use of AI", "location": "cover"},
    {"id": "c_responsible_ai", "name": "Responsible AI use", "aliases": ["responsible use of AI"], "description": "Responsible, equitable, traceable, reliable, and governable use of AI by federal agencies and other entities.", "grounding_span": "responsible, equitable, traceable, reliable, and governable AI capabilities", "location": "p.1"},
    {"id": "c_governance", "name": "Governance", "aliases": ["governance principle"], "description": "Principle describing key practices to promote accountability by establishing processes to manage, operate, and oversee AI implementation.", "grounding_span": "Governance. This principle describes key practices to promote accountability by establishing processes to manage, operate, and oversee AI implementation.", "location": "p.9"},
    {"id": "c_data", "name": "Data", "aliases": ["data principle"], "description": "Principle describing key practices to help entities use data appropriate for the intended use of each AI system.", "grounding_span": "Data. This principle describes key practices to help entities use data that are appropriate for the intended use of each AI system.", "location": "p.9"},
    {"id": "c_performance", "name": "Performance", "aliases": ["performance principle"], "description": "Principle describing key practices to help entities produce results consistent with program objectives.", "grounding_span": "Performance. This principle describes key practices to help entities produce results that are consistent with program objectives.", "location": "p.9"},
    {"id": "c_monitoring", "name": "Monitoring", "aliases": ["monitoring principle"], "description": "Principle describing key practices to help entities ensure their AI systems remain reliable and relevant over time.", "grounding_span": "Monitoring. This principle describes key practices to help entities ensure their AI systems remain reliable and relevant over time.", "location": "p.9"},
    {"id": "c_ai_lifecycle", "name": "AI life cycle", "aliases": ["life cycle of an AI system"], "description": "The lifecycle of an AI system comprising four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring.", "grounding_span": "The life cycle of an AI system involves four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring.", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_phase_design", "name": "Design", "aliases": [], "description": "First phase of the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_phase_development", "name": "Development", "aliases": [], "description": "Second phase of the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_phase_deployment", "name": "Deployment", "aliases": [], "description": "Third phase of the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_phase_monitoring", "name": "Continuous monitoring", "aliases": [], "description": "Fourth phase of the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_third_party_audits", "name": "Third-party assessments and audits", "aliases": ["robust and independent audits"], "description": "Independent assessments and audits important to achieving responsible, equitable, traceable, reliable, and governable AI.", "grounding_span": "Third-party assessments and audits are important to achieving these goals.", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "c_oversight_community", "name": "Auditors and the oversight community", "aliases": ["third-party assessors", "oversight community"], "description": "Actors who play a vital role in evaluating AI under a trust-but-verify approach.", "grounding_span": "Auditors and the oversight community play a vital role in the “trust but verify” equation", "location": "p.10"},
    {"id": "c_trust_but_verify", "name": "Trust but verify", "aliases": [], "description": "Verification posture toward evaluating AI technology.", "grounding_span": "“trust but verify” equation", "location": "p.10"},
    {"id": "c_digital_services", "name": "Digital services", "aliases": [], "description": "Delivery of digital information and transactional services across platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms.", "grounding_span": "Federal agencies rely on digital services to interact with the public and improve organizational performance.", "location": "p.5"},
    {"id": "c_digital_expertise", "name": "Digital expertise", "aliases": ["digital services staff", "digitally-ready workforce", "digital workforce"], "description": "In-house digital and government-related skills needed to design, develop, deploy, use, and monitor AI systems.", "grounding_span": "a critical mass of workforce expertise is needed to enable federal agencies to accelerate the delivery and adoption of AI", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "c_digital_academy", "name": "Digital services academy", "aliases": ["Federal Academy"], "description": "A proposed new service academy, similar to military academies, to train future civil servants in digital competencies.", "grounding_span": "the federal government establish a new service academy—similar to the military academies—to train future civil servants in the digital competencies needed to modernize government", "location": "p.6"},
    {"id": "c_high_risk", "name": "High-risk area (federal workforce skills gaps)", "aliases": [], "description": "Mission-critical gaps in federal workforce skills and expertise that GAO has designated a high-risk area since 2001.", "grounding_span": "GAO has identified mission-critical gaps in federal workforce skills and expertise in science and technology as high-risk areas since 2001", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "c_machine_learning", "name": "Machine learning", "aliases": ["machine learning systems"], "description": "Model-building approach used within the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "building one or more machine learning", "location": "p.2"},
    {"id": "c_ai_technical_stakeholders", "name": "AI technical stakeholders", "aliases": [], "description": "Data scientists, data engineers, developers, cybersecurity specialists, program managers, and others responsible for technical performance.", "grounding_span": "AI technical", "location": "p.3"},
    {"id": "c_community_stakeholders", "name": "Community of stakeholders", "aliases": ["broader community of participants"], "description": "Policy and legal experts, subject matter experts, and individuals using or impacted by an AI system, engaged in AI development.", "grounding_span": "a broader community of participants—policy and legal \nexperts, subject matter experts, and individuals using the AI system or impacted by its use", "location": "p.4"},
    {"id": "c_societal_concerns", "name": "Societal concerns", "aliases": ["societal implications", "societal consequences"], "description": "Non-technical impacts, including bias and equity, that must be identified and mitigated when implementing AI.", "grounding_span": "identifying and mitigating any societal concerns", "location": "p.3"},
    {"id": "c_kp_workforce", "name": "Workforce (governance key practice)", "aliases": [], "description": "Recruit, develop, and retain personnel with multidisciplinary skills across the AI life cycle.", "grounding_span": "Workforce: Recruit, develop, and retain personnel with multidisciplinary skills and \nexperiences in design, development, deployment, assessment, and monitoring of AI \nsystems.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_clear_goals", "name": "Clear goals", "aliases": [], "description": "Define clear goals and objectives for the AI system to ensure intended outcomes.", "grounding_span": "Clear goals: Define clear goals and objectives for the AI system to ensure intended \noutcomes are achieved.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_roles", "name": "Roles and responsibilities", "aliases": [], "description": "Define clear roles, responsibilities, and delegation of authority for the AI system.", "grounding_span": "Roles and responsibilities: Define clear roles, responsibilities, and delegation of authority", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_values", "name": "Values", "aliases": [], "description": "Demonstrate a commitment to values and principles established by the entity to foster public trust.", "grounding_span": "Values: Demonstrate a commitment to values and principles established by the entity to \nfoster public trust in responsible use of the AI system.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_stakeholder", "name": "Stakeholder involvement", "aliases": [], "description": "Include diverse perspectives from a community of stakeholders throughout the AI life cycle to mitigate risks.", "grounding_span": "Stakeholder involvement: Include diverse perspectives from a community of \nstakeholders throughout the AI life cycle to mitigate risks.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_risk_mgmt", "name": "Risk management", "aliases": [], "description": "Implement an AI-specific risk management plan to systematically identify, analyze, and mitigate risks.", "grounding_span": "Risk management: Implement an AI-specific risk management plan to systematically \nidentify, analyze, and mitigate risks.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_specifications", "name": "Specifications", "aliases": [], "description": "Establish and document technical specifications to ensure the AI system meets its intended purpose.", "grounding_span": "Specifications: Establish and document technical specifications to ensure the AI system \nmeets its intended purpose.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_compliance", "name": "Compliance", "aliases": [], "description": "Ensure the AI system complies with relevant laws, regulations, standards, and guidance.", "grounding_span": "Compliance: Ensure the AI system complies with relevant laws, regulations, standards, \nand guidance.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_transparency", "name": "Transparency", "aliases": [], "description": "Promote transparency by enabling external stakeholders to access information on the design, operation, and limitations of the AI system.", "grounding_span": "Transparency: Promote transparency by enabling external stakeholders to access \ninformation on the design, operation, and limitations of the AI system.", "location": "p.11"},
    {"id": "c_kp_sources", "name": "Data sources", "aliases": [], "description": "Document sources and origins of data used to develop the models underpinning the AI system.", "grounding_span": "Sources: Document sources and origins of data used to develop the models", "location": "p.12"},
    {"id": "c_kp_reliability", "name": "Data reliability", "aliases": ["Reliability"], "description": "Assess reliability of data used to develop the models.", "grounding_span": "Reliability: Assess reliability of data used to develop the models.", "location": "p.12"},
    {"id": "c_data_quality", "name": "Data quality", "aliases": ["high-quality data"], "description": "The need for high-quality data and algorithms in machine learning and AI systems.", "grounding_span": "the need for high-quality data and algorithms in", "location": "p.12"},
    {"id": "c_bias", "name": "Bias", "aliases": ["biases", "inequities"], "description": "Potential biases, inequities, and other societal concerns resulting from an AI system or its data.", "grounding_span": "Bias: Identify potential biases, inequities, and other societal concerns resulting from the \nAI system.", "location": "p.13"},
    {"id": "c_kp_security_privacy", "name": "Security and privacy", "aliases": [], "description": "Assess data security and privacy for the AI system.", "grounding_span": "Security and privacy: Assess data security and privacy for the AI system.", "location": "p.12"},
    {"id": "c_performance_metrics", "name": "Performance metrics", "aliases": [], "description": "Performance metrics that are precise, consistent, and reproducible.", "grounding_span": "Define performance metrics that are precise, consistent, and reproducible.", "location": "p.13"},
    {"id": "c_kp_human_supervision", "name": "Human supervision", "aliases": [], "description": "Define and develop procedures for human supervision of the AI system to ensure accountability.", "grounding_span": "Human supervision: Define and develop procedures for human supervision of the AI \nsystem to ensure accountability.", "location": "p.13"},
    {"id": "c_drift", "name": "Data and model drift", "aliases": ["data drift", "model drift", "Drift"], "description": "The acceptable range of data and model drift that must be established to ensure desired results.", "grounding_span": "Drift: Establish the range of data and model drift that is acceptable to ensure the AI \nsystem produces desired results.", "location": "p.14"},
    {"id": "c_traceability", "name": "Traceability", "aliases": [], "description": "Document results of monitoring activities and corrective actions to promote traceability and transparency.", "grounding_span": "Traceability: Document results of monitoring activities and any corrective actions taken \nto promote traceability and transparency.", "location": "p.14"},
    {"id": "c_scaling", "name": "Scaling", "aliases": ["expanded use"], "description": "Identify conditions under which the AI system may be scaled or expanded beyond its current use.", "grounding_span": "Scaling: Identify conditions, if any, under which the AI system may be scaled or expanded \nbeyond its current use.", "location": "p.14"},
    {"id": "c_ai_governance_structure", "name": "AI governance structure", "aliases": ["governance structures for AI systems"], "description": "Governance structures that incorporate organizational values, consider risks, assign clear roles, and involve multidisciplinary stakeholders.", "grounding_span": "Entities should implement governance structures for AI systems that \nincorporate organizational values, consider risks, assign clear roles and \nresponsibilities, and involve multidisciplinary stakeholders.", "location": "p.11"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "def_digital_services", "term": "Digital services", "verbatim_text": "Such digital services, as defined by the Office of Management and Budget, include the delivery of digital information (e.g., data or content) and transactional services (e.g., online forms) across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms, such as websites, mobile applications, and social media.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-05-16", "grounding_span": "as defined by the Office of Management and Budget, \ninclude the delivery of digital information (e.g., data or content) and transactional services (e.g., \nonline forms) across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms, such as \nwebsites, mobile applications, and social media.", "location": "p.5"},
    {"id": "def_ai_lifecycle", "term": "Life cycle of an AI system", "verbatim_text": "The life cycle of an AI system involves four phases: design, development, deployment, and continuous monitoring.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2023-05-16", "grounding_span": "The life cycle of an AI system involves four phases: design, development, deployment, and \ncontinuous monitoring.", "location": "p.2"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_rapid_pace", "claim_text": "Artificial intelligence (AI) is evolving at a rapid pace and the federal government cannot afford to be reactive to its complexities, risks, and societal consequences.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Artificial intelligence (AI) is evolving at a rapid pace and the federal government \ncannot afford to be reactive to its complexities, risks, and societal consequences.", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "cl_workforce_needed", "claim_text": "A critical mass of workforce expertise is needed to enable federal agencies to accelerate the delivery and adoption of AI.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "a critical mass of workforce \nexpertise is needed to enable federal agencies to accelerate the delivery and \nadoption of AI.", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "cl_severe_shortage", "claim_text": "As the federal government continues its modernization efforts, it faces a severe shortage of digital expertise, including in the field of AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "it \nfaces a severe shortage of digital expertise, including in the field of AI.", "location": "p.1"},
    {"id": "cl_2000_positions", "claim_text": "One roundtable participant noted that their agency had more than 2,000 open positions requiring digital skill sets.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "one roundtable participant \nnoted that their agency had more than 2,000 open positions requiring digital skill sets", "location": "p.6"},
    {"id": "cl_comp_not_competitive", "claim_text": "Current salaries and compensation for federal digital services staff are not competitive with the private sector.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Current salaries and compensation for federal \ndigital services staff are not competitive with the private sector.", "location": "p.8"},
    {"id": "cl_audits_important", "claim_text": "Third-party assessments and audits are important to achieving responsible, equitable, traceable, reliable, and governable AI.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Third-party assessments and audits are \nimportant to achieving these goals.", "location": "highlights"},
    {"id": "cl_high_risk_since_2001", "claim_text": "Since 2001, GAO has identified mission-critical gaps in federal workforce skills and expertise as high-risk areas.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Since 2001, however, GAO has identified mission-critical gaps in federal workforce skills and expertise", "location": "p.5"},
    {"id": "cl_infrastructure_limits", "claim_text": "A lack of modern technology infrastructure limits the ability of government agencies to leverage the skills of digital services staff.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Participants said a lack of modern technology \ninfrastructure limits the ability of government agencies to leverage the skills of digital \nservices staff.", "location": "p.8"}
  ],

    {"type": "precedes", "from_id": "c_phase_development", "to_id": "c_phase_deployment", "grounding_span": "design, development, deployment, and \ncontinuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"type": "precedes", "from_id": "c_phase_deployment", "to_id": "c_phase_monitoring", "grounding_span": "design, development, deployment, and \ncontinuous monitoring", "location": "p.2"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c_machine_learning", "to_id": "c_ai", "grounding_span": "building one or more machine learning \nmodels", "location": "p.2"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "def_digital_services", "grounding_span": "as defined by the Office of Management and Budget", "location": "p.5"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "def_ai_lifecycle", "grounding_span": "The life cycle of an AI system involves four phases", "location": "p.2"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_rapid_pace", "grounding_span": "Artificial intelligence (AI) is evolving at a rapid pace and the federal government \ncannot afford to be reactive", "location": "highlights"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_workforce_needed", "grounding_span": "a critical mass of workforce \nexpertise is needed", "location": "highlights"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_severe_shortage", "grounding_span": "it \nfaces a severe shortage of digital expertise", "location": "p.1"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_2000_positions", "grounding_span": "their agency had more than 2,000 open positions requiring digital skill sets", "location": "p.6"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_comp_not_competitive", "grounding_span": "Current salaries and compensation for federal \ndigital services staff are not competitive with the private sector.", "location": "p.8"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_audits_important", "grounding_span": "Third-party assessments and audits are \nimportant to achieving these goals.", "location": "highlights"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_high_risk_since_2001", "grounding_span": "GAO has identified mission-critical gaps in federal workforce skills and expertise", "location": "p.5"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "gao-ai-accountability-framework-ariga-testimony", "to_id": "cl_infrastructure_limits", "grounding_span": "a lack of modern technology \ninfrastructure limits the ability of government agencies to leverage the skills of digital \nservices staff.", "location": "p.8"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "In 2020, the Department of Defense (DOD) \nestablished an AI Executive Steering Group, which \nwas created as the senior governance body to \nprovide coordination and oversight of DOD's AI \npolicies and activities.", "suggested_edge": "exemplifies", "note": "DOD AI Executive Steering Group is offered as a concrete example of the Governance principle / AI governance structure; the schema has no example/exemplifies edge from an organizational entity to a Concept."},
    {"grounding_span": "This testimony summarizes two related \nreports—GAO-22-105388 and GAO-\n21-519SP.", "suggested_edge": "summarizes", "note": "This testimony summarizes two underlying GAO reports; 'summarizes' is stronger/more specific than a plain cites edge and is not expressible."},
    {"grounding_span": "Foundational to \nsolving the AI accountability challenge is having a critical mass of digital expertise", "suggested_edge": "is_prerequisite_for", "note": "Digital expertise is asserted as foundational/prerequisite to solving the AI accountability challenge; no dependency edge exists between two Concepts."},
    {"grounding_span": "Participants also highlighted \nchallenges that federal agencies are facing, such as having a need for technical expertise, a \nlimited understanding of how AI makes its decisions, and limited access to key information due \nto commercial procurement of such systems.", "suggested_edge": "impedes", "note": "Commercial procurement / limited explainability is described as impeding AI oversight; no barrier/impedes edge type is available."}
  ]
}
